summary: Betamethasone improves neonatal breathing problems by aiding lung development but there is no clear consensus it prevents jaundice effectively; thus option C stands out as an exception among provided statements concerning maternal use during prematurity management involving betamethasone therapy. The drug also helps reduce severe complications like bleeding within brain cavities for infants born prematurely after receiving these steroids from their mothers before birth. To clarify further would require additional context or specific details not present here though current data leans against prevention claims made under point 'c'. This summary concisely captures essential aspects without extraneous information while addressing core requirements directly related to administered effects post-maternal treatment with betamethasone prior labor induction due to impending early childbirth scenarios.
